name: Montage: Beat 8: The Long Road Home

text.content:
Beat 8: The Long Road Home

Act: Act 1 | Type: Montage Scene

Overview

The Return Journey

Level: 2→3 | Beat: 8 | Location: Green Heart → Ashen Woods (or Frozen Steppe) → Interior Belt → Midgard's Shield → Hrafnborg approach

~220 miles. ~12 days. The party returns west with Kaelen and Lew in tow. The world has deteriorated since the outbound trek. The dead move in organised columns toward Hrafnborg. Grafvitnir's network is closing.

Difficulty: Moderate (the route is known; the risks are different) | Rounds: 2 | Adjusted for: 4 heroes + 2 NPC companions

NPC Contributions:

  • Lew: Assists one PC's test per round (Intuition +4 or Reason +2). Navigation less reliable (eye is dim), but survival skills compensate.
  • Kaelen: Assists one PC's test per round (Might +3 or Intuition +2). Strong, practical, observant. Drives the pace.

Outcomes

  • Total Success (5+ before 3 failures): Swift and efficient return. Arrive near Hrafnborg with supplies, energy, and time. Undead column is behind them. Earn 2 Milestones.
  • Partial Success: Arrive at cost — choose: rations gone (starving by the time they sight Hrafnborg); time cost (undead column is ahead, Hrafnborg already under pressure); or injury cost (one PC arrives with 1 fewer Hit Die). Earn 1 Victory.
  • Total Failure: All PCs and NPCs lose 1 Hit Die. Undead army has reached Hrafnborg—the siege has begun and the PCs are outside the walls. No Milestones. Director gains 4 Malice for the Act 1 climax.

Running This Scene

This is a narrative montage — a series of challenges the party faces as a group. Rather than a single roll, play this out as a sequence of scenes where each character contributes.

GM Guidance

  • Go around the table and ask each player how their character is helping.
  • Call for ability checks as appropriate — suggested modified [Resolute←13] (moderate).
  • Let creative approaches succeed with good roleplaying, even without a roll.
  • Use the complications below as prompts — not every one needs to come up.
  • The goal is collaborative storytelling, not a pass/fail test.

Relevant Skills:

  • Strong
  • Quick or Discreet
  • Cunning or Cunning
  • Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant
  • Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive

Complications & Challenges

Use these as dramatic beats during the montage. Present them as situations the party must navigate together.

Round 1: The Wasteland Exit (Days 1–4)

Navigate Back Through the Ashen Woods (Cunning or Cunning, modified [Resolute←13])

The charcoal forest has shifted since the outbound trek—trunks have fallen, ash drifts have changed, and the root network beneath is more active. Retrace the route with constant adjustments as the landscape refuses to stay the same.

Pace Through Worsening Weather (Strong, modified [Resolute←13])

The temperature has dropped since the outbound trek. Frost now rimes the charcoal trunks each morning. Wind carries ash and ice crystals both. What was merely cold before is now a constant physical drain.

Avoid Blighted Beasts (Agility / Intuition)

The Rot-Beasts and Vine Stranglers are more active now, driven by the cold toward the Green Heart's warmth. The party crosses their territory going the wrong direction—give them a wide berth or face a fight the party cannot afford.

Find Usable Shelter (Cunning or Cunning, modified [Resolute←13])

The shelters from the outbound trek—the Charcoal Cathedral, the root-caves—may be occupied (by beasts or by newcomers). Finding somewhere safe to camp requires scouting and improvisation in hostile terrain.

Read the Weather Signs (Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant, modified [Resolute←13])

Storm systems move differently in Fimbulwinter. Reading cloud patterns, wind shifts, and animal behaviour (such as it is) can mean the difference between pushing on or hunkering down safely in advance of a killer squall.

Keep Kaelen Moving (Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive, modified [Resolute←13])

Kaelen is mourning. Leaving the Green Heart cost her. She moves slowly, stops often, stares southeast. The PCs need to keep her engaged without being cruel about it—she will break if pushed wrong, and she's too important to break.

Escalation

If the scene needs more tension, introduce these additional complications:

Round 2: The Interior Belt & Mountain Crossing (Days 5–12)

Phase 1 — The Interior Belt (Days 5–8)

Navigate Through Refugee-Clogged Roads (Reason / Presence)

The roads used outbound are packed with refugees heading north—away from Hrafnborg. Word has spread that the dead are gathering. The PCs head directly into the flow, fighting the current of desperate humanity.

Forage in a Depleted Landscape (Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant, modified [Resolute←13])

The Dead Farms have been picked clean by refugees and worse. Finding food, fuel, or shelter requires creativity—old root cellars, frozen caches, hunting what little game survives in the grey wasteland.

Cross a Collapsing Bridge (Agility / Might)

The river bridge used outbound has partially collapsed—supports rotted by the grey water. Options: repair it (Reason modified [Resolute←14]), climb across debris (Agility modified [Resolute←12]), or ford the river (Might modified [Resolute←13] but costs Heat from cold exposure).

Avoid Undead Patrol Columns (Agility / Intuition)

The dead move in organised columns now—dozens of draugr walking southwest toward Hrafnborg. They don't hunt the PCs specifically, but their routes intersect. A wrong turn puts the party in a column's path. The columns take twenty minutes to pass.

Maintain Supply Discipline (Cunning or Cunning, modified [Resolute←13])

Twelve days is long. Eight days in, supplies are thinning. Rationing now affects morale (Presence tests at second chance (two rolls, take worst) if rations are cut) but preserves options for the mountain crossing.

Keep the Party Together Emotionally (Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive, modified [Resolute←13])

Kaelen is volatile. Lew is worried about Kaelen. The PCs are tired. Managing morale and preventing friction between NPCs and heroes is its own challenge—and failure ripples into combat readiness.

Phase 2 — The Mountain Crossing (Days 9–12)

Navigate the Titan's Stair in Blizzard Conditions (Cunning or Cunning, modified [Resolute←13])

The switchback trail up the mountain is buried under fresh snow. Ice has glazed the stone steps. Visibility is twenty feet on a good hour, five feet in the gusts. Navigation is by memory, guesswork, and Lew's faltering compass-sense.

Endure the Blizzard (Strong, modified [Resolute←13])

The worst cold the party has faced. Wind-chill drops temperatures to lethal ranges. Characters without proper winter gear risk frostbite (ongoing cold damage) and exhaustion. This is the first true blizzard of early winter.

Clear the Rockslide at the Narrows (Might / Reason)

A rockslide (warned about by Thyra at Greyfen Holdfast) has narrowed the pass to single-file. Clearing it requires brute force, engineering, or both. Failure means climbing over loose scree with full packs—dangerous for everyone.

Spot the Chainwarden Checkpoint (or Its Absence) (Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant, modified [Resolute←13])

The Chainwarden toll-post at Greypass should be visible from the upper switchbacks. If the PCs look: the post is dark. The fire is out. No one is there. Abandoned but not attacked—Eirik evacuated south on the King's orders. The system is collapsing inward.

Manage the Descent in Ice (Quick or Discreet, modified [Resolute←13])

The north face of Midgard's Shield is sheeted in ice. The descent is treacherous—a single slip sends a character sliding hundreds of feet down frozen rock. Rope work, ice-picks, or magical solutions needed. This is where the exhausted make fatal mistakes.

Rally for the Final Push (Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive, modified [Resolute←13])

Twelve days of march. Blizzard. Cold. Depleting supplies. The party can see Hrafnborg from the mountaintop—a flickering amber glow on the horizon. But between them and the city: open ground, draugr columns, and the worst of the storm. One last push.

Outcomes

Based on how the party performed, use the following as a guide:

Round 1 Interlude: The World is Worse — Part I

The clearing where they camped outbound is different. The trunks they used as windbreaks have fallen. The fire-pit is full of grey ice. And on the stone where they left an offering for Solveig—nothing. The offering is untouched. No, not untouched: gone. In its place, a charcoal stick broken in half.

(If Bone Fields route outbound: the barrows they sealed are open again. Every one. The capstones pushed aside from the inside. The bones inside are gone.)

Point of Interest: The Dead Waystation

At a crossroads, a stone shelter with a turf roof. Inside: bodies. Seven people—a family. They died huddled together around a fire that went out. Their supplies are scattered but mostly intact. The elder's hand still holds a knife. Tally marks on the floor: fourteen days' worth. They waited for help that never came.

If the PCs bury the family (2 hours, no mechanical benefit): Kaelen helps dig. Lew finds a prayer-stone in the medicine box and places it on the cairn.

Round 2 Interlude — The World is Worse (Part II)

A village they passed outbound is empty—not abandoned, empty. Doors open, food on tables, no people, no bodies. Footprints in frost lead southwest toward the draugr columns.

At one point, the party passes a column of draugr walking southwest—dozens of them, three abreast, shields hanging loose. They take twenty minutes to pass. None look at the party. Then Kaelen's ember-mark flares, casting a shadow—and two or three draugr turn their heads. Slowly, mechanically. "Don't do that again," says Lew.

Round 2 Negotiation: The Holdfast at Greyfen

Before Round 3, the party encounters a fortified refugee camp led by former Chainwarden sergeant Thyra Blackhand. Moderate negotiation (Interest 3, Patience 2). She wants news from the south, confirmation Hrafnborg stands, and help with an incoming draugr column. She provides passage, 3 days' trail rations, and intelligence: the Titan's Stair has a rockslide at the narrows. If Kaelen reveals her ember-mark: automatic success (Interest +2).

Round 2 Interlude — The View from the Summit

At the summit of Titan's Stair, the wind drops for a moment. Looking south: Hrafnborg. The Ever-Ember's light is visible even through the storm—a warm amber glow, impossibly small. But around it: darkness. A stain on the land, surrounding the city. Kaelen's ember-mark blazes. "It's still lit. But it's screaming. Something cold and old and patient is pressing against it."

Total Success

Swift and efficient return. Arrive near Hrafnborg with supplies, energy, and time. Undead column is behind them. Kaelen is shaken but steady. Earn 2 Milestones.

Partial Success

Arrive at cost. Choose one: rations gone (starving by the time Hrafnborg is sighted); time cost (undead column is ahead—the city is already under pressure on arrival); or one PC arrives with 1 fewer Hit Die (fall, rockslide, or frostbitten limb). Earn 1 Victory.

Total Failure

All PCs and NPCs lose 1 Hit Die. The undead army has already reached Hrafnborg. The siege has begun and the PCs are outside the walls. No Milestones. Director gains 4 Malice for the Act 1 climax.
